title: Changing the default expression for a column should materialize the old default in the old parts
description:
### Company or project name
ClickHouse
### Use case
There is a quirky, but well-known behavior: after adding a new column and then changing the default expression for a column, it will also change its values in the data parts where the column was not present.
Example:
```
CREATE TABLE test (x UInt8) ORDER BY x;
INSERT INTO test (x) VALUES (1);
ALTER TABLE test ADD COLUMN y String DEFAULT 'Hello';
SELECT * FROM test ORDER BY x;
INSERT INTO test (x) VALUES (2);
SELECT * FROM test ORDER BY x;
ALTER TABLE test MODIFY COLUMN y String DEFAULT 'World';
SELECT * FROM test ORDER BY x;
INSERT INTO test (x) VALUES (3);
SELECT * FROM test ORDER BY x;
```
```
CREATE TABLE test
(
`x` UInt8
)
ORDER BY x
Query id: 979c2c17-b19e-4535-942d-f6b252d87751
Ok.
0 rows in set. Elapsed: 0.039 sec.
INSERT INTO test (x) FORMAT Values
Query id: 9050fe54-abaa-4d78-ac21-9a07e2c602f7
Ok.
1 row in set. Elapsed: 0.004 sec.
ALTER TABLE test
(ADD COLUMN `y` String DEFAULT 'Hello')
Query id: e73e87e7-6380-4662-8dc5-544a16507ebd
Ok.

SELECT *
FROM test
ORDER BY x ASC

Ok.

SELECT *
FROM test
ORDER BY x ASC
